an5.114:1.1: At one time, the _Bhagavā_ was dwelling among the Magadhans, at Andhakavinda.
an5.114:1.2: Then the venerable Ānanda approached the _Bhagavā_; having approached and paid homage to the _Bhagavā_, he sat to one side. To the venerable Ānanda seated to one side, the _Bhagavā_ said this:
an5.114:2.1: "Those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, who are new, recently gone forth, newly arrived in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] — those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, should be encouraged, settled, and established in five *dhammas*.
an5.114:2.3: 'Come, friends, be virtuous, dwell restrained with the restraint of the *Pātimokkha*, endowed with conduct and resort, seeing danger in the slightest faults, and train by undertaking the training rules' — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in *Pātimokkha* restraint.
an5.114:3.1: 'Come, friends, dwell with the doors of your *indriyas* guarded, with *sati* that is watchful and careful, endowed with a protected mind, with a mind [*citta*] guarded by *sati*' — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in restraint of the *indriyas*.
an5.114:4.1: 'Come, friends, be of little speech, and set limits on talk' — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in limiting talk.
an5.114:5.1: 'Come, friends, be forest-dwellers, frequent remote lodgings in forests and woodland' — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in bodily seclusion.
an5.114:6.1: 'Come, friends, have right view, be endowed with right seeing' — thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in right seeing.
an5.114:6.2: Those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, who are new, recently gone forth, newly arrived in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] — those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, should be encouraged, settled, and established in these five *dhammas*."
